panels.title: Peak detector — predicted vs realized
panels.description: Whole-home grid power (15-min mean, InfluxDB) with the optimizer's predicted capacity-peak slots overlaid as red bands and the predicted peak threshold as a dashed line. Scoreline on top: a past predicted slot counts as a hit when it lands in the realized top-K 15-min draws of the visible window (K = number of predicted slots already past); misses = top-K draws that were not predicted, false alarms = past predicted slots outside the top-K. Peak-slot data since 2026-07-03 (v0.2.0) — until predictions age past their slot time the bands sit in the future and no score can be computed. Grid power history in InfluxDB starts 2026-06-28.

targets.query:
hist = from(bucket: "zeus") |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop) |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "zeus_grid_power_w" and r._field == "value")
live = from(bucket: "zeus") |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop) |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "zeus_state" and r._field == "grid_power_w")
union(tables: [hist, live])
  |> group()
  |> aggregateWindow(every: 15m, fn: mean, createEmpty: false)
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({_time: r._time, _value: r._value / 1000.0}))
  |> keep(columns: ["_time", "_value"])
  |> rename(columns: {_value: "Grid power (kW)"})
  |> sort(columns: ["_time"])

panels.title: Forecast bias — weekday × hour (kWh)
panels.description: Signed mean forecast error per Brussels weekday × hour: max(zeus_forecast_load_kwh offset 1h) − max(zeus_realized_load_kwh), attributed to the hour the load actually occurred. Blue = over-forecast, red = under-forecast, white ≈ unbiased. Hover a cell for the signed kWh and the sample count. Prometheus retention is ~10 days, so most cells average only 1–2 samples — expect noise until more history accumulates.

panels.title: Daily forecast error trend (MAE / MAPE)
panels.description: Rolling 1-day window, stepped hourly. MAE = mean |realized − forecast| in kWh per hourly slot (forecast shifted 1h to align with the slot it predicted). MAPE = same error relative to realized load, with realized clamped to ≥ 0.05 kWh to guard against division by zero; right axis. Lower is better.

panels.title: Forecast vs actual (7d)
panels.description: Hourly load: realized (zeus_realized_load_kwh, reported for the last completed slot) vs the forecast made for that same slot (zeus_forecast_load_kwh offset 1h). The two lines should track each other; gaps between them are forecast error. Fixed 7-day window regardless of the dashboard time range.

panels.title: Predicted savings calibration
panels.description: CAVEAT — not apples-to-apples: zeus_predicted_savings_eur is a rolling 36h-horizon prediction, while realized savings accrue per calendar day. The prediction is scaled to a per-day rate (× 24/36) as a defensible approximation; the green curve is zeus_savings_today_eur, which resets at midnight and accumulates through the day. Calibration check: each day's end-of-day green peak should land near the dashed predicted €/day line that preceded it. Systematic gaps mean the optimizer over- or under-promises.

panels.title: Frozen forecast vs realized — InfluxDB (long history)
Frozen forecast vs realized — critical load (hourly kWh)
panels.description: InfluxDB, unlimited retention (unlike the Prometheus panels above, ~10d). Realized = zeus_load_history.kwh (hourly critical-load energy). Frozen forecast = zeus_forecast_frozen.load_kwh — written ONCE per 15-min slot from the cycle one slot before it and never revised, so it is the honest at-the-time forecast (lead ≈ 15 min); per-slot values × 4 = hourly kWh. Plan forecast = zeus_forecast.load_kwh, rewritten over the whole horizon every cycle (its persisted value is the last nearest-term write) — shown for contrast and for the forward horizon. Frozen data starts 2026-07-01; both frozen and realized queries group() across the site_id tag transition (untagged history + site_id=tervuren since 2026-07-04).

targets.query:
from(bucket: "zeus")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "zeus_load_history" and r._field == "kwh")
  |> group()
  |> aggregateWindow(every: 1h, fn: mean, createEmpty: false, timeSrc: "_start")
  |> keep(columns: ["_time", "_value"])
  |> rename(columns: {_value: "Realized (kWh/h)"})
  |> sort(columns: ["_time"])

from(bucket: "zeus")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "zeus_forecast_frozen" and r._field == "load_kwh")
  |> group()
  |> aggregateWindow(every: 1h, fn: mean, createEmpty: false, timeSrc: "_start")
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({_time: r._time, _value: r._value * 4.0}))
  |> rename(columns: {_value: "Frozen forecast (kWh/h)"})
  |> sort(columns: ["_time"])

from(bucket: "zeus")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "zeus_forecast" and r._field == "load_kwh")
  |> group()
  |> aggregateWindow(every: 1h, fn: mean, createEmpty: false, timeSrc: "_start")
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({_time: r._time, _value: r._value * 4.0}))
  |> rename(columns: {_value: "Plan forecast (kWh/h)"})
  |> sort(columns: ["_time"])

panels.title: Rolling MAE — frozen forecast vs realized (1d / 7d / 30d)
panels.description: InfluxDB, unlimited retention. Hourly |frozen forecast − realized| for the critical load, smoothed with trailing windows of 1d / 7d / 30d. Frozen data starts 2026-07-01, so the 7d and 30d lines only become meaningful as history accrues. Lower is better; the 1d line reacting while 7d/30d stay flat = a transient miss, all three rising = model drift.

targets.query:
import "experimental"
lookback = experimental.subDuration(d: 1d, from: v.timeRangeStart)
fr = from(bucket: "zeus")
  |> range(start: lookback, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "zeus_forecast_frozen" and r._field == "load_kwh")
  |> group()
  |> aggregateWindow(every: 1h, fn: mean, createEmpty: false, timeSrc: "_start")
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({_time: r._time, frozen: r._value * 4.0}))
re = from(bucket: "zeus")
  |> range(start: lookback, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "zeus_load_history" and r._field == "kwh")
  |> group()
  |> aggregateWindow(every: 1h, fn: mean, createEmpty: false, timeSrc: "_start")
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({_time: r._time, realized: r._value}))
join(tables: {f: fr, r: re}, on: ["_time"])
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({_time: r._time, _value: if r.frozen - r.realized >= 0.0 then r.frozen - r.realized else r.realized - r.frozen}))
  |> sort(columns: ["_time"])
  |> timedMovingAverage(every: 1h, period: 1d)
  |> unique(column: "_time")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._time >= v.timeRangeStart)
  |> rename(columns: {_value: "MAE 1d (kWh)"})

import "experimental"
lookback = experimental.subDuration(d: 7d, from: v.timeRangeStart)
fr = from(bucket: "zeus")
  |> range(start: lookback, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "zeus_forecast_frozen" and r._field == "load_kwh")
  |> group()
  |> aggregateWindow(every: 1h, fn: mean, createEmpty: false, timeSrc: "_start")
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({_time: r._time, frozen: r._value * 4.0}))
re = from(bucket: "zeus")
  |> range(start: lookback, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "zeus_load_history" and r._field == "kwh")
  |> group()
  |> aggregateWindow(every: 1h, fn: mean, createEmpty: false, timeSrc: "_start")
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({_time: r._time, realized: r._value}))
join(tables: {f: fr, r: re}, on: ["_time"])
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({_time: r._time, _value: if r.frozen - r.realized >= 0.0 then r.frozen - r.realized else r.realized - r.frozen}))
  |> sort(columns: ["_time"])
  |> timedMovingAverage(every: 1h, period: 7d)
  |> unique(column: "_time")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._time >= v.timeRangeStart)
  |> rename(columns: {_value: "MAE 7d (kWh)"})

import "experimental"
lookback = experimental.subDuration(d: 30d, from: v.timeRangeStart)
fr = from(bucket: "zeus")
  |> range(start: lookback, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "zeus_forecast_frozen" and r._field == "load_kwh")
  |> group()
  |> aggregateWindow(every: 1h, fn: mean, createEmpty: false, timeSrc: "_start")
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({_time: r._time, frozen: r._value * 4.0}))
re = from(bucket: "zeus")
  |> range(start: lookback, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "zeus_load_history" and r._field == "kwh")
  |> group()
  |> aggregateWindow(every: 1h, fn: mean, createEmpty: false, timeSrc: "_start")
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({_time: r._time, realized: r._value}))
join(tables: {f: fr, r: re}, on: ["_time"])
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({_time: r._time, _value: if r.frozen - r.realized >= 0.0 then r.frozen - r.realized else r.realized - r.frozen}))
  |> sort(columns: ["_time"])
  |> timedMovingAverage(every: 1h, period: 30d)
  |> unique(column: "_time")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._time >= v.timeRangeStart)
  |> rename(columns: {_value: "MAE 30d (kWh)"})

panels.title: Rolling bias — frozen forecast − realized (1d / 7d / 30d)
panels.description: InfluxDB, unlimited retention. Signed hourly error (frozen forecast − realized) for the critical load, smoothed with trailing windows of 1d / 7d / 30d. Above zero = the model over-forecasts, below = under-forecasts; a persistent offset in the 7d/30d lines is systematic bias the fit should be absorbing. Frozen data starts 2026-07-01.

targets.query:
import "experimental"
lookback = experimental.subDuration(d: 1d, from: v.timeRangeStart)
fr = from(bucket: "zeus")
  |> range(start: lookback, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "zeus_forecast_frozen" and r._field == "load_kwh")
  |> group()
  |> aggregateWindow(every: 1h, fn: mean, createEmpty: false, timeSrc: "_start")
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({_time: r._time, frozen: r._value * 4.0}))
re = from(bucket: "zeus")
  |> range(start: lookback, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "zeus_load_history" and r._field == "kwh")
  |> group()
  |> aggregateWindow(every: 1h, fn: mean, createEmpty: false, timeSrc: "_start")
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({_time: r._time, realized: r._value}))
join(tables: {f: fr, r: re}, on: ["_time"])
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({_time: r._time, _value: r.frozen - r.realized}))
  |> sort(columns: ["_time"])
  |> timedMovingAverage(every: 1h, period: 1d)
  |> unique(column: "_time")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._time >= v.timeRangeStart)
  |> rename(columns: {_value: "Bias 1d (kWh)"})

import "experimental"
lookback = experimental.subDuration(d: 7d, from: v.timeRangeStart)
fr = from(bucket: "zeus")
  |> range(start: lookback, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "zeus_forecast_frozen" and r._field == "load_kwh")
  |> group()
  |> aggregateWindow(every: 1h, fn: mean, createEmpty: false, timeSrc: "_start")
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({_time: r._time, frozen: r._value * 4.0}))
re = from(bucket: "zeus")
  |> range(start: lookback, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "zeus_load_history" and r._field == "kwh")
  |> group()
  |> aggregateWindow(every: 1h, fn: mean, createEmpty: false, timeSrc: "_start")
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({_time: r._time, realized: r._value}))
join(tables: {f: fr, r: re}, on: ["_time"])
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({_time: r._time, _value: r.frozen - r.realized}))
  |> sort(columns: ["_time"])
  |> timedMovingAverage(every: 1h, period: 7d)
  |> unique(column: "_time")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._time >= v.timeRangeStart)
  |> rename(columns: {_value: "Bias 7d (kWh)"})

import "experimental"
lookback = experimental.subDuration(d: 30d, from: v.timeRangeStart)
fr = from(bucket: "zeus")
  |> range(start: lookback, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "zeus_forecast_frozen" and r._field == "load_kwh")
  |> group()
  |> aggregateWindow(every: 1h, fn: mean, createEmpty: false, timeSrc: "_start")
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({_time: r._time, frozen: r._value * 4.0}))
re = from(bucket: "zeus")
  |> range(start: lookback, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "zeus_load_history" and r._field == "kwh")
  |> group()
  |> aggregateWindow(every: 1h, fn: mean, createEmpty: false, timeSrc: "_start")
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({_time: r._time, realized: r._value}))
join(tables: {f: fr, r: re}, on: ["_time"])
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({_time: r._time, _value: r.frozen - r.realized}))
  |> sort(columns: ["_time"])
  |> timedMovingAverage(every: 1h, period: 30d)
  |> unique(column: "_time")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._time >= v.timeRangeStart)
  |> rename(columns: {_value: "Bias 30d (kWh)"})

panels.title: Realized load by target — critical / A/C plug / whole-home grid
panels.description: The forecaster's training targets side by side (InfluxDB). Critical load = zeus_load_history.kwh (hourly). A/C plug = zeus_load_history.ac_kwh — the two_component forecaster's second target, accruing to InfluxDB only since 2026-07-04 (JUPITER P2.1), so it is sparse. Whole-home grid = mean grid draw per hour (zeus_grid_power_w history ∪ zeus_state.grid_power_w, /1000) ≈ kWh/h; note this is grid IMPORT, i.e. consumption net of battery/PV flows, not true whole-home consumption. Only the critical-load forecast is persisted (zeus_forecast/zeus_forecast_frozen carry load_kwh only), so forecast-vs-realized scoring above is critical-load only — the A/C and whole-home targets have no persisted forecast series yet.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "zeus")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "zeus_load_history" and r._field == "kwh")
  |> group()
  |> aggregateWindow(every: 1h, fn: mean, createEmpty: false, timeSrc: "_start")
  |> keep(columns: ["_time", "_value"])
  |> rename(columns: {_value: "Critical load (kWh/h)"})
  |> sort(columns: ["_time"])

from(bucket: "zeus")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "zeus_load_history" and r._field == "ac_kwh")
  |> group()
  |> aggregateWindow(every: 1h, fn: mean, createEmpty: false, timeSrc: "_start")
  |> keep(columns: ["_time", "_value"])
  |> rename(columns: {_value: "A/C plug (kWh/h)"})
  |> sort(columns: ["_time"])

hist = from(bucket: "zeus")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "zeus_grid_power_w" and r._field == "value")
live = from(bucket: "zeus")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "zeus_state" and r._field == "grid_power_w")
union(tables: [hist, live])
  |> group()
  |> aggregateWindow(every: 1h, fn: mean, createEmpty: false, timeSrc: "_start")
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({_time: r._time, _value: r._value / 1000.0}))
  |> rename(columns: {_value: "Whole-home grid (kWh/h)"})
  |> sort(columns: ["_time"])

panels.title: Training window + forecast source
panels.description: zeus_training_window_days = span of the load history the forecaster was fit on this cycle; a sharp drop means InfluxDB returned a partial read and the CSV fallback was unavailable — the forecaster is training on too short a window. Forecast source (right axis, 0 = in-process fit, 1 = jupiter forecast-service) shows which fit served each cycle — context for the #124 parity checkpoint. Prometheus, ~10d retention.

panels.title: Capacity peak — predicted horizon peak vs billed monthly peak (kW)
panels.description: Predicted horizon peak = zeus_predicted_peak_kw (Prometheus, ~10d retention; 0 when peak shaving is off) — the highest whole-home grid-import power the forecaster sees over the coming horizon. Billed monthly peak = zeus_state.capacity_peak_kw (InfluxDB, unlimited retention) — the running peak the capacity tariff bills on; it steps up when a 15-min draw sets a new monthly record and resets each month. The peak-slot hit-rate scoreline (predicted slots vs realized top-K draws) lives in the top panel of this dashboard.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "zeus")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "zeus_state" and r._field == "capacity_peak_kw")
  |> group()
  |> aggregateWindow(every: 1h, fn: max, createEmpty: false, timeSrc: "_start")
  |> keep(columns: ["_time", "_value"])
  |> rename(columns: {_value: "Billed monthly peak (kW)"})
  |> sort(columns: ["_time"])

options.content:
### Reserved for the central-vs-in-process parity panels (card #124, P2 checkpoint)

Planned here once the parity checkpoint lands:

- served-forecast delta: jupiter forecast-service vs in-process fit, same cycle / same slots
- parity MAE and worst-slot delta over the soak window
- `zeus_forecast_source` flips and `zeus_forecast_model_age_seconds` during the soak
- backtest-oracle score, once available

**Known instrumentation gaps** (why some #131 panels are absent from this dashboard):

- **Temp-slope drift** (cooling kWh/°C): `TempAwareForecaster` only *logs* its fitted slope — it is not exported to Prometheus or persisted to InfluxDB, so no drift panel is possible yet. Needs a small zeus change (gauge or `zeus_state` field) first.
- **Per-forecaster MAE split** (TempAware / TwoComponent / Baseline): persisted forecasts carry no tag or field identifying which fit served, so rolling error cannot be attributed per forecaster.
- **Whole-home & A/C forecasts are not persisted** — `zeus_forecast` / `zeus_forecast_frozen` carry only the critical-load `load_kwh`, so forecast-vs-realized scoring is critical-load only.
